steps.title: Identify the described limitation
steps.content: The passage names the research's critical problem: “a myopic focus on a few countries.” This directly signals what the research claimed to have—comprehensive coverage.

steps.title: Match comprehensiveness to the word choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean comprehensive or complete coverage, since the research "claimed" this but actually had a "myopic focus on a few countries." Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — novelty: newness or originality — researchers might claim novelty, but the passage's criticism is about geographic scope, not originality. ✗
  • Choice B — immutability: the quality of being unchanging — researchers do not typically claim their income inequality data is immutable; this does not contrast with a myopic focus. ✗
  • Choice C — exhaustiveness: complete, thorough coverage — directly contrasts with "myopic focus on a few countries"; researchers claimed to cover everything but did not. ✓
  • Choice D — pervasiveness: widespread presence — describes how broadly something exists, not whether a study is comprehensive in its coverage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
